title: cornus mas var. aurea-elegantissima
intro: Cornus mas var. aurea-elegantissima, also known as the Golden Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Cornus genus and is native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is believed to have various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The bark and leaves of the Golden Dogwood can be used in herbal preparations, often in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Cornus mas var. aurea-elegantissima for medicinal purposes.
